llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm available: ai_assisted, which pools all thirteen AI models. This is compared against the control arm with no AI assistance. The design is within-subject as participants completed tasks both with and without AI.
llm_extracted.notes: Within-subject design where participants completed tasks both with and without AI. Sample size n=1499 is found in Table 2 (Appendix A). The SD can be calculated from the standardized effect: the paper reports 0.14 standard deviations for a 0.2-point difference, which implies SD ≈ 0.2/0.14 ≈ 1.43. This is consistent with the Root MSE = 1.3865 reported in Table 4 for the quality outcome. Using SD = 1.4 as a conservative estimate based on these values.
source_quotes: The average grade without access to an AI model was 4.51. When participants were able to use an AI model this increased to 4.71, an increase of 0.2 points on a seven-point scale. This equates to an increase of 0.14 standard deviations in the average grade.

computed.notes: Within-subject design where participants completed tasks both with and without AI. Sample size n=1499 is found in Table 2 (Appendix A). The SD can be calculated from the standardized effect: the paper reports 0.14 standard deviations for a 0.2-point difference, which implies SD ≈ 0.2/0.14 ≈ 1.43. This is consistent with the Root MSE = 1.3865 reported in Table 4 for the quality outcome. Using SD = 1.4 as a conservative estimate based on these values.
